plans.label: attendance.summary_read
plans.sql: SELECT total FROM `tabEvent Attendance Summary` WHERE branch=%s AND event=%s

plans.label: attendance.count_star
plans.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `tabFlock Attendance Record` WHERE branch=%s AND event=%s

plan.Extra: Using intersect(event,branch); Using where; Using index

plans.label: attendance.filter_unseen
plans.sql: SELECT event, attendee_ref, client_req_id FROM `tabFlock Attendance Record` WHERE (event, attendee_ref, client_req_id) IN ((%s, %s, %s), (%s, %s, %s), (%s, %s, %s))

plan.Extra: Impossible WHERE noticed after reading const tables

plans.label: registration.waitlist_count
plans.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM `tabFlock Event Registration` WHERE gathering=%s AND registration_status='Waitlisted'

plans.label: realtime.gathering_branch_resolve
plans.sql: SELECT branch FROM `tabFlock Gathering` WHERE name=%s

plans.label: attendance.branch_scoped_list
plans.sql: SELECT name, attendee_ref, event, status FROM `tabFlock Attendance Record` WHERE branch=%s ORDER BY modified DESC LIMIT 100

issues.evidence: 3 separate SELECTs for branch+group+org per registration; register_for_event + process_bulk_batch call all three.

issues.path: Branch-scoped attendance list (Flock Attendance Record list view)
issues.evidence: EXPLAIN picks the `modified` index scanning 1427 rows with 'Using where' instead of the branch index — the ORDER BY modified DESC overrides the branch filter. ~18ms median at 3k rows/branch.
issues.fix: Composite index (branch, modified DESC) so the filter + sort share one index; or deferred sort.

issues.evidence: _bump does read-then-write (get_value + set_value) for checked_in_count — non-atomic; loses updates under concurrent check-in AND costs 2 queries where 1 suffices. Compare _bump_registered_count which correctly uses UPDATE ... = count + 1.
Each member in a bulk batch runs SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the gathering row → 500 serial row locks per batch. Serializes the 15k bulk-registration path.
issues.fix: Decide capacity once per batch under a single lock, not per member.

issues.evidence: Loops invitee_groups calling group_subtree (recursive tree query) + a per-group get_value(expires_on). O(invitations × subtree depth) at eligibility-check time.
issues.fix: Bulk-resolve subtrees once; fold expiry into the invitation fetch.

issues.evidence: Branch-total aggregate fetches every event summary row then Python sum() — grows with event count per branch.
issues.fix: SELECT SUM(total) ... WHERE branch=%s at the DB.

throttle.note: In-memory primitive; production Redis backend shares the throttle_allows contract (FLO-319).
